package: Goal 13: Non-Clifford And Scrambling Bridge-Channel Controls

scope.family: encoded Bell resources with product five-qubit right-mouth blocks

core_claim: Coarse/low-order entropy shadows do not determine naive bridge-channel transfer; algebra-aware Clifford and non-Clifford controls restore transfer, while mouth-blind scrambling controls fail to produce structured named-mouth capacity.

theorem_style_claims.claim: Aligned and twisted encoded-mouth resources have matching coarse entropy/min-cut and labeled physical entropy through the code distance, but identity activation has different transfer capacity.
A public algebra-aware activation followed by logical T has full bridge-channel capacity and a non-Clifford Pauli-transfer matrix; a T-dagger phase frame restores identity fidelity.
A mouth-blind Pauli-twirled scrambling proxy has zero structured named-mouth Pauli transfer and zero structured transfer capacity on the representative resources and bounded atlas.

bounded_exhaustive_evidence.scope: aligned and twisted two-mouth encoded resources with identity, algebra-aware Clifford, algebra-aware T-dressed, and mouth-blind twirled controls
all resource pairings for m<=3 under identity, algebra-aware Clifford, algebra-aware T-dressed, and mouth-blind twirled controls

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the Goal 13 non-Clifford/scrambling bridge-channel control certificate.
Regression check for the Goal 13 certificate.

diagnostic_hierarchy.low_order_physical_entropy_through_dista: insufficient for the encoded mouth map
diagnostic_hierarchy.algebraic_connectivity_plus_public_activ: determines transfer in the declared finite family
diagnostic_hierarchy.non_clifford_pauli_transfer: distinguishes T-dressed transfer from Clifford identity transfer
diagnostic_hierarchy.mouth_blind_scrambling_control: fails to produce structured named-mouth transfer
diagnostic_hierarchy.otoc_support_growth: detects structured receiver support for bridge activations but not for the twirled control

known_vs_new.conceptual_prior_art: Engelhardt-Liu 2023, Algebraic ER=EPR and Complexity Transfer, is the primary prior art for the broad operator-algebraic ER=EPR framing.
known_vs_new.known_derived: Standard stabilizer distance, encoded Bell transfer, Clifford Pauli-transfer bookkeeping, logical T channel action, and Pauli twirling.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: Packages a finite bridge-channel control certificate where low-order entropy-matched encoded resources split under naive transfer, algebra-aware Clifford and non-Clifford controls restore capacity, and mouth-blind scrambling controls fail.

limitations: This is an exact finite encoded-QEC benchmark. The non-Clifford control is a logical T-dressed transfer, and the generic scrambling control is an exact mouth-blind Pauli-twirled proxy, not a chaotic many-body simulation or continuum-gravity theorem. Full encoded-block diagnostics reveal the mouth map.
next_frontier: Replace the Pauli-twirled proxy with richer finite many-body dynamics or tensor-network scramblers and test whether algebraic bridge connectivity still predicts transfer better than entropy shadows.
